name: every source rule is enforceable (binds a native detector) and at least 90% also bind a routed 3rd-party library, the rest being native-only structural detectors
command: ruby -ryaml -rjson -rset -e 'man=JSON.parse(File.read(ARGV[1]))["tools"].map{|t| t["tool"]}.to_set; total=0; routed=0; Dir[ARGV[0]+"/*/*.yaml"].each{|f| d=YAML.unsafe_load_file(f) rescue next; next unless d.is_a?(Hash); (d["rules"]||[]).each{|r| total+=1; eb=r["enforced_by"]||[]; routed+=1 if eb.any?{|b| (b["tool"] && man.include?(b["tool"])) || b["bundle"]}}}; STDERR.puts("total=#{total} routed=#{routed}"); exit((total>0 && routed>=(total*90/100)) ? 0 : 1)' "$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT/generated-code-quality-standards" "$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT/rubric/runners/toolchain.json" && echo most-rules-bind-thirdparty 1>&2
